_cottage.swimVerdict: Direct beach access from lower level. Sandy beach with kayaks and paddleboard included. Campfire on the shore. Long walks on the beach.
_cottage.notes: Wall of windows with lake views. Open concept. Main floor: Queen bed (BR1), Single-over-Queen bunk (BR2), 4pc bath. Lower level: Queen bed (BR3), 3pc bath, entertainment space, beach access. Max 7 occupancy. 1 dog allowed. Noise monitoring device. Parking for 3. Beautifully landscaped.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lakeview beachfront with stone collecting. Clean water - not weedy or slimy. Bring water shoes for rocky shore. Great sunset views.
_cottage.notes: Gorgeous sunset views. Open concept living. Main floor: Queen bed (BR2), Single-over-Double bunk (BR3), 4pc bath, laundry. Upper level has master bedroom. Stone beach - great for geology lovers but bring water shoes. Max 7 occupancy. 2 dogs allowed. Noise monitoring. Parking for 2. License: BWSTR25-181.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lakefront with beach under a willow tree. Sandy/stony beach. Outdoor shower. World-class sunsets. Beach varies by season.
_cottage.notes: Named after the willow tree on the beach. Sunroom with views on 3 sides. Outdoor kitchen with beverage fridge, sink and BBQ with live edge bar overlooking lake. Premier BR with Queen, BR2 with Queen, BR3 with single-over-single bunk. Outdoor shower. Playground. Max 6 occupancy. 1 dog. Parking for 4. License: BWSTR25-188. Picturesque Bayfield Main Street nearby.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake view cottage with beach access. Good for families with playful backyard. Cozy evenings by the fire. Beach vacation vibes.
_cottage.notes: Spacious 4-bedroom cottage. King bed (premier BR), Double + Twin-over-Queen bunk (BR2), 2 twin beds (BR3), Double bed (BR4). Hot tub. Electric fireplace. Open layout. Playful backyard. 2 dogs allowed. Max 8 occupancy. Parking for 4. Farthest from Diana & Klaus in Sarnia. Kincardine/Inverhuron area - further north along Lake Huron.

_cottage.swimVerdict: 1-minute walk to the lake. Sandy beach. Perfect for families or couples. Modern A-frame design with brand new outdoor sauna.
_cottage.notes: Brand new stylish A-frame cottage. Premier King bed, Queen bedroom, Double-over-Double bunk + Twin. 2 bathrooms. EV charger on-site. Newly added outdoor sauna. 2 honeymoon balconies. 1-minute walk to lake. 10 mins from Grand Bend & Bayfield. Max 8 occupancy. 2 dogs allowed. License: BWSTR25-004.

_cottage.swimVerdict: NO stairs to beach. Beachfront retreat on well-treed private lot. Sandy beaches, historic lighthouse, biking trails, and Lake Huron sunsets. Perfect for families.
_cottage.notes: Beachfront cottage with NO STAIRS to beach - rare find! 4BR + loft, sleeps 10. BR1: Queen (access through BR2), BR2: Double, BR3: Double, BR4: Single + Single-over-Single bunk, Loft: 4 Double beds. 3 bathrooms. Hot tub. 2 kayaks + 2 paddleboards. Point Clark area near historic lighthouse. Max 10 occupancy. 2 pets allowed.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lakefront with floor-to-ceiling windows facing Lake Huron. New stairs added in 2025 to lake access. 80 stairs to water. World-renowned sunsets.
_cottage.notes: Stunning floor-to-ceiling windows facing Lake Huron. 5 min south of Bayfield village. Premier King bed in loft, Queen bedroom, Single over Single + Single over Double bunks. Den with pullout Double bed. 2 bathrooms. 80 stairs to water (new stairs added 2025). Screened porch. Max 8 occupancy. 1 non-shedding dog allowed. License: BWSTR25-134.

_cottage.swimVerdict: 2-minute walk to sandy beach with NO STAIRS. Perfect for mobility issues. Wagon provided for beach chairs. Sandy lakebed.
_cottage.notes: Single level home with only 2 stairs to enter - wheelchair accessible! 2-minute walk to sandy beach with NO STAIRS. Perfect for mobility issues. 3BR: King, Queen, Queen. 2 bathrooms. Gazebo over dining area. Outdoor firetable. Max 6 occupancy. 1 small hypoallergenic dog allowed. License: BWSTR25-178.

_cottage.swimVerdict: 1-minute walk to rocky beach or 5-minute bike ride to sandy beach. Modern, beautifully updated cottage across from Lake Huron.
_cottage.notes: Modern, beautifully updated cottage in Point Clark. 3BR+: King, King, Queen, plus Den with fold-out Double. 2 bathrooms. Hot tub. Games room with air hockey table. 5 adult & 3 kids bicycles. 1-minute walk to rocky beach. Sun loungers, hanging egg chair. Max 8 occupancy. 2 pets allowed.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Approximately 80 steps down to beach. Sandy/stony lakebed - water shoes recommended. Stunning lakeview deck perfect for gathering.
_cottage.notes: High-end quality finishes. 3+BR: King (main floor), Queen (upstairs), 2 Doubles (upstairs), plus extra room with Single bed + Crib. 3 full bathrooms + powder room. Games room with ping pong table in garage. Large deck with retractable awning. High-end kitchen with wine fridge. 2 kayaks. 80 steps to beach. Max 8 occupancy. 1 small non-shedding dog allowed.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake view and lake access from backyard. Floor-to-ceiling windows with lake views. Hot tub. Kayak, paddleboard & lake mat included.
_cottage.notes: Charming lakeside retreat near Bayfield. 4BR: Queen (main), Double (main), Double (2nd level), Single over Single bunk (2nd level). 1 bathroom. Hot tub. Floor-to-ceiling windows with lake views. Kayak, paddleboard & lake mat included. Volleyball/badminton net. Large deck. Max 8 occupancy. 2 dogs allowed. License: BWSTR25-022.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Direct river access with 2-minute paddle to beach, or short 2-minute walk to sandy beach (no stairs). Modern stylish cottage.
_cottage.notes: Stylish modern cottage near Inverhuron. 2BR + Loft: 2 Queen beds + King bed in loft. 1 bathroom. Open concept with floor-to-ceiling windows. Kitchen with induction stove and live-edge island. Direct river access with 2-minute paddle to beach, or short 2-minute walk to sandy beach (no stairs). 2 kayaks + 2 paddleboards. Near Inverhuron Provincial Park, Kincardine Lighthouse & Museum. 2 min to Tiverton & Bruce Power, 10 min to Kincardine. Max 6 occupancy. 2 pets allowed.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Steps to sandy Lake Huron beach, shallow entry with some stones then sandy, never busy
_cottage.notes: Port Albert village, 15 min from Goderich. 3BR: master (Q) main floor + 2 upstairs (2Q + 2 singles). Walk-out basement with ping-pong. Backyard overlooks Nine Mile River. NO PETS. 5.0/5 rating (5 reviews). Families only, no one under 25. $500 security deposit. **JULY: 2 weeks available (Jul 5-18)**

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake Huron just around the corner, close to sandy beach
_cottage.notes: Laid-back Lake Huron cottage with calm blue tones throughout. 2+BR/2BTH, sleeps 8. Pets allowed (2, with addendum). Max occupancy monitored. Primary renter must be 25+. **JULY: ~3.5 weeks open (Jul 1-26)**

_cottage.swimVerdict: Steps from Point Clark Lighthouse — one of Lake Huron's best sandy beaches
_cottage.notes: Freshly renovated pet-friendly retreat steps from Point Clark Lighthouse — one of Lake Huron's best sandy beaches. Private fenced yard, firepit, classic cottage charm. 2BR/1BTH, sleeps 6 (smaller side). **JULY: 4 weeks open (Jul 1-27)**

_cottage.swimVerdict: Near Lake Huron with big yard, beach vibes but not lakefront
_cottage.notes: 3BR + carriage house, sleeps 8. Big yard with games, beach vibes. Near Lake Huron but not lakefront. Pet friendly (1 non-shedding dog). ⚠️ July availability is fragmented — only ~2.5 weeks total, not continuous. **BORDERLINE pick.**

_cottage.swimVerdict: 50 feet of private sandy beach with gentle sloping entry into Georgian Bay. Crystal clear water, excellent for swimming and snorkeling. Views of Meaford Bluffs and Blue Mountains from the cottage.
_cottage.notes: TRUE WATERFRONT gem on Georgian Bay. 4 bedrooms all with queen beds. Pet-friendly ($10/day). Private sandy beach is the real deal. Owner Doug highly rated. A second 2-bedroom cottage next door can be rented for overflow. Cleaning fee $175. $400 refundable security deposit. Reviews: 4.8/5 stars. Best waterfront value in the Georgian Bay region at $2,200/week.

_cottage.swimVerdict: 5-minute walk to beautiful Meaford beaches on Georgian Bay. Multiple beaches within walking distance. Not waterfront but very close.
_cottage.notes: Modern, beautifully upgraded vacation home in the HEART of Meaford. Walking distance to beach, marina, shops, restaurants, LCBO. 37 high-quality photos on listing. Near Wasaga Beach, Sauble Beach, Owen Sound, Collingwood/Blue Mountain. $200-400/night (~$2,100/week estimated). Fully equipped with life-size Connect 4 and corn hole games. Great for families. Natural gas BBQ means never running out. Excellent reviews.

discoveries.name: House of Lords - Georgian Bay Waterfront

_cottage.swimVerdict: Flat shoreline with shallow entry into remarkably clear Georgian Bay water. Excellent snorkeling. Swim raft provided. Safe for kids with gradual entry. Crystal clear water typical of the Wiarton/Bruce Peninsula area.
_cottage.notes: Charming waterfront cottage with BUNKIE for extra sleeping. 3 bedrooms + bunkie with queen bed = sleeps 8. Flat shoreline is ideal for kids. Clear water for snorkeling. 20 min from Wiarton. Boat docking available at Wiarton Marina. Boat launch at Big Bay 6km away. Pillows/blankets provided but bring your own linens/towels. Price estimated at ~$2,000/week for this category on Cottage Vacations.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Less than 1-MINUTE WALK to Sauble Beach — 11km of pristine sandy beach on Lake Huron. Warm shallow water, world-class sunsets. One of Ontario's best swimming beaches.
_cottage.notes: Classic Sauble Beach cottage on a corner lot. 1 King, 1 Queen, 1 Double, 2 sets of single bunks + 2 pullout couches. Great for larger families. Pool table and trampoline keep kids busy. Price estimated ~$2,200/week for Sauble Beach in peak season. Managed by Sauble Beach Cottage Rentals (reputable local agency).

_cottage.swimVerdict: 5-minute walk to Sauble Beach's famous 11km sandy shoreline on Lake Huron. Warm, shallow water perfect for families. World-class sunsets.
_cottage.notes: PREMIUM cottage on quiet crescent with exquisite landscaping. Bright and airy two-story. Central AC is a big plus for July heat. 3 Queen beds + bunk beds + pullout sofa. Two separate living areas. Covered back deck. Well-maintained grounds. No pets allowed. This is the upscale pick for Sauble Beach.

_cottage.swimVerdict: 10-minute walk to Lake Huron's sandy beach, plus minutes from Silver Lake shoreline. Two water bodies to choose from. Beach wagon and chairs provided. 2 kayaks + canoe included for water exploration.
_cottage.notes: Charming cottage nestled in trees with abundant privacy. Cathedral ceiling with stone propane fireplace. 2 Queen beds + single-over-double bunk. Access to BOTH Silver Lake and Lake Huron. The included kayaks and canoe are a huge bonus — explore Sauble by water. PS4 for rainy days. Budget-friendly at estimated $1,800/week. The best value pick for Sauble Beach.

discoveries.name: Out of the Blue - Family Cottage

_cottage.swimVerdict: 5-minute walk to Port Elgin's beautiful sandy beach on Lake Huron. North Shore Park Splash Pad nearby for kids. Sheltered swimming areas. Stunning sunsets over the lake.
_cottage.notes: EXCELLENT VALUE at $2,100/week summer rate ($1,250 off-season). 15 photos on listing. 4 bedrooms: 2 queens + 2 double/single bunks. Fenced backyard big enough for badminton/volleyball. Walking distance to downtown, shopping, restaurants, beaches. 6 reviews all 5-stars. Bring your own linens/towels. $300 refundable deposit. Closest region to Sarnia at just 2h 15min drive. Near Diana & Klaus.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Directly across the road from Miramichi Bay on Lake Huron. Sandy beach access steps away. Located on the Great Lakes Waterfront Trail for walking/biking along the lakeshore.
_cottage.notes: Newly renovated 4-season cottage BETWEEN Port Elgin and Southampton — best of both towns. 5 bedrooms (3 main floor + 2 basement) with 2 full baths. Basement has rec room with wood stove. Direct access to Woodland Trail from backyard. On the Great Lakes Waterfront Trail. Pet-friendly. Families or quiet groups only. World-famous Lake Huron sunsets from front deck. Estimated ~$2,000/week.

_cottage.swimVerdict: 1-minute walk to Beach Area 4 — one of the best stretches of Wasaga's 14km of sandy shoreline (longest freshwater beach in the world). Warm, shallow Georgian Bay water. Dog-friendly Beach 3 also within walking distance.
_cottage.notes: PROVEN WINNER — 8 reviews averaging 4.7/5 stars with multiple families rebooking year after year. Quiet dead-end street, no through traffic. Close to park for kids. Owner Oswald is responsive and accommodating. 15-20 min to Blue Mountain/Collingwood. Veranda with bay views. 5G WiFi is excellent. The most reviewed and trusted Wasaga option.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Steps from Beach Area 4 on Georgian Bay — 14km of pristine sandy shoreline. Safe family-friendly beach with no public parking on the street (quiet). Kids can play freely. Listen to waves from the front patio.
_cottage.notes: Same great location as VFamily Cottage but newer listing. 3-bedroom bungalow with bright open living space and hardwood floors. Dead-end street with no public parking = extra safe for kids. Walk to Tim Hortons/McDonald's. 15-20 min to Blue Mountain. Can be rented alongside the front cottage for extended family groups up to 20. Budget-friendly at $1,000-2,000/week.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Sandy beach access via ~90 shared stairs (flights of 6-8 steps with platforms). Shared with sister cottage Shades of Blue.
_cottage.notes: Classic rustic 3-season lakefront cottage. 2BR main cottage + 2BR bunkie. Max 8 people. 2.3km from Bayfield downtown. No linens/towels provided. $500 refundable security deposit. Sister cottage to Shades of Blue. Beds: BR1 single bunks (sleeps 2), BR2 queen (2), BR3 double (2), BR4 single-over-double bunk + single (3).

_cottage.swimVerdict: Directly on Lake Huron waterfront with spacious lot. Double-wide lot backing right onto the water.
_cottage.notes: 3 bedrooms + bunkie house with bunk beds. 2 full bathrooms. Double wide lot with three decks. Directly on Lake Huron waterfront. Host named Heather — described as excellent. Reviews praise spaciousness exceeding photos. 5 min to Bayfield, 15 min to Grand Bend.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake access with variable lakebed — sandy or stoney depending on conditions. Sand typically found 3ft out. Good for stone collecting. Not weedy or slimy. Water shoes recommended.
_cottage.notes: 3BR, 1 bath, sleeps 6. Max 2 dogs allowed (pet addendum required). Parking limited to 3 vehicles. Noise/temp/wifi monitoring device. Primary renter must be over 25. Parties strictly prohibited. Max occupancy 6 at all times.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake access via approx 45 stairs to beach. Includes kayaks, paddleboard & lake mat for water activities.
_cottage.notes: Standout property — newly added AC, panoramic window cedar barrel sauna (wood provided), EV charger. Upstairs master with NEW king bed, 2 downstairs queens. Upstairs + downstairs 3pc bathrooms with showers. Well-equipped kitchen with espresso maker, Ninja blender, ice maker. 10 min from Bayfield. Max 5 occupancy. License #BWSTR25-063.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lakefront property designed to take advantage of lake views from every angle — kitchen, fireplace, hot tub, gazebo, and beach. Direct lake view and access.
_cottage.notes: Open concept with reclaimed barn wood beams, thick stone walls, full wall of windows. Hot tub with lake views. Designed for immersive lakefront experience. Max occupancy listed as 4 on rental terms (original brief says sleeps 5). Parking limited to 3 vehicles. License #BWSTR25-054.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake view and lake access. Exceptional Bayfield beach cottage with stunning lake views inside and out.
_cottage.notes: 2BR, 1 bath, sleeps 4. Queen bed in master. Hot tub. Gas fireplace. Stunning lake views from inside. Full-size appliances, plenty of counter space. Max 1 well-behaved dog. Professionally managed by Jennifer at CottageStays. License #BWSTR25-072.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake view and lake access. Unforgettable sunsets from the property.
_cottage.notes: 2BR, 1.5 bath, sleeps 5. King bed — great for couples. Hot tub. Bright interiors. Perfect for family adventures or romantic escapes. Max 1 dog allowed. May have outdoor camera. License #BWSTR25-156.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lakefront with lots of beach! Lower sunset deck with seating overlooking lake. Direct lake view and lake access. Paddleboard & lake mat rentals available mid-June to mid-Sept.
_cottage.notes: Vintage lakefront cottage with character. 2BR (both double beds) + pullout sofa. Half bathroom inside + outdoor shower only (no full indoor shower). Wood fireplace — real cottage feel. Lower sunset deck. Parking limited to 2 vehicles. Max 1 small pet. Playpen/highchair available on request. Kitchen: stove, oven, microwave, toaster, drip coffee maker, kettle, fridge.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Private beach access via short walk down the hill. Uncrowded beach. Parking at beach can be arranged for those who can't manage the incline.
_cottage.notes: Step above usual cottage — described as 'conveniences of home with comfort of an excellent hotel.' Queen + double/single bunk beds (sleeps 5 max). Premium mattresses, fine linens & towels PROVIDED (unique among listings). Air conditioned. At end of dead-end road — very quiet. Hosts June & Keith nearby but leave you alone. 2h25min from Toronto. Pet fee C$60. C$400 pre-auth deposit. Also listed on CottagesInCanada (DI-25253) and TripAdvisor.

discoveries.name: Cozy  Cabin #3 one block to beach

discoveries.name: Cozy Cabin #1, 1 block to the beach.

_cottage.notes: Mar 16, 2026 · Entire guesthouse · Prime location in the village of Grand Bend   1 block to Ontario's most beautiful beach, enjoy Canada's best sunset! Rustic and cozy cabin, firepit...

discoveries.name: Cottage in Grand Bend with rooftop patio

_cottage.notes: Mar 16, 2026 · Entire cottage · AGE: 25 or older age requirement !!!  cottage is 3 blocks to the main beach 3 blocks to the main strip 2 short blocks to Highway 21. Located in a q...
Mar 16, 2026 · Entire guesthouse · Prime location in the village of Grand Bend 1 block to Ontario's most beautiful beach enjoy  Canada's best sunset. , fire pit, patio, bbq, flat top...

discoveries.name: Romantic Couples Getaway - Sauna, Hot Tub, Private Beach access, Hiking trails
Cozy, rustic cottage minutes to a private beach
Homey Cottage Studio on Lake Huron for Week-Long Getaway near Grand Bend, Ontario
Luxury Studio Rental in a Charming Cottage on Lake Huron near Grand Bend, Ontario
Romantic Couples Getaway Sauna, Hot Tub, Private Beach access, Hiking trails
8 Minutes to Huron Sand Beaches. Prettiest Town Goderich
Blue Skies House - Cool Games Room
Cozy Bayfield Retreat • Pool & Hot Tub • 15min to Grand Bend • Lake Huron Nearby
Beutt's Beach House - Private Lake Access
Private Lake View Cottage on Lake Huron
2 bedroom small cottage 1 minute to beach OPTIONAL bunkie with queen bed

_cottage.swimVerdict: Private island setting in the 30,000 Islands. Deep-water dock with crystal clear Georgian Bay water. Rocky shoreline but excellent swimming off the dock. Incredible isolation - true wilderness feel.
_cottage.notes: PRIORITY: Remote island in Parry Sound archipelago. Most private setting possible. Bring your own boat or arrange pickup. Generator power (quiet). Perfect for those seeking true disconnection. Price reflects premium remote location.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Classic Pointe au Baril location on the scenic channel. Deep water at dock, clear Georgian Bay water. Boat traffic on channel but peaceful setting otherwise. Famous area for fishing and boating.
_cottage.notes: Classic Georgian Bay cottage area. Famous marine heritage. Good access to open bay. Area has stores and restaurant. ~2.5h from Toronto.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Crystal clear water on Georgian Bay's north shore near Killarney. Famous La Cloche mountains as backdrop. Rocky but pristine swimming - the water is remarkably clear. Snorkeling paradise.
_cottage.notes: PRIORITY: Near Killarney Provincial Park and the famous CP rail bridge. World-class scenery. The La Cloche Silhouette is iconic. ~3.5h from Toronto but worth every minute.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Private sandy beach on a quiet Haliburton lake. Gradual entry perfect for kids. Clean, refreshing swimming. Many lakes in the area have excellent water quality.
_cottage.notes: PRIORITY: Excellent value in Haliburton. Quieter than Muskoka but still beautiful lakes. Great for families. Artistic community - check out the Sculpture Forest. ~2.5h from Toronto.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Redstone Lake - known for excellent water clarity. Sandy beach with shallow entry. Quiet, wooded setting. Popular for kayaking and paddleboarding.
_cottage.notes: Redstone Lake is a gem in Haliburton. Clear water, good swimming, not as busy as Muskoka. Multiple good lakes in the area. ~2.5-3h from Toronto.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Lake Manitou - one of the largest inland lakes in Ontario. Clean water, sandy beach. Remote island setting with incredible privacy. The Mindemoya Lake area also has good options.
_cottage.notes: PRIORITY: Most remote option - largest freshwater island in the world. Great for getting away from it all. 4.5h drive + ferry or bridge crossing. Unique experience. Budget-friendly.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Sandy beach on Sharbot Lake in the Frontenac Arch. Clean swimming, shallow entry. Close to Kingston (1.5h from Toronto). Great value for the quality.
_cottage.notes: PRIORITY: Best value in the bunch. Closest to Toronto (~2h). The Frontenac Arch is a UNESCO biosphere. Quiet lakes, good swimming, excellent value. Perfect for shorter trips.

_cottage.swimVerdict: Classic Canadian Shield setting on one of the Land O' Lakes region lakes. Deep water off dock, clear lake water. Rock and pine aesthetic. Very quiet area.
_cottage.notes: The Land O' Lakes region (aka adding 'LoL' for fun) has many beautiful lakes. More remote than Sharbot but still doable from Toronto. Typical Canadian Shield - rocks, pines, clear water. ~2.5h.

discoveries.name: 1233 Yonge Street
discoveries.address: 1233 Yonge St & 9 Woodlawn Ave E, Toronto, ON (SE corner Yonge & Woodlawn)

_development.developer: Plazacorp / Firm Capital (operating as 1233Y Development LP)

discoveries.name: Yonge & Birch Residences
discoveries.address: 1196-1210 Yonge St & 2-8 Birch Ave, Toronto, ON (NW corner Yonge & Birch)

_development.status: Revised OPA/SPA returned to City Council (Nov 2025)

discoveries.name: 135 St Clair Ave W
discoveries.address: 135 St Clair Ave W, Toronto, ON (SE corner St Clair & Avenue Rd)

_development.status: Proposal filed, facing strong local opposition

discoveries.name: 1220 Yonge Street
discoveries.address: 1220 Yonge St, Toronto, ON (SW corner Yonge & Alcorn)

discoveries.name: Central Restaurants
discoveries.notes: 4.8★/871 reviews. Right on Ossington, steps from Golden Turtle. Top rated on the strip.

discoveries.name: La Cubana
discoveries.notes: 4.4★/1828 reviews. Cuban, 92 Ossington — essentially next door to Lunch Lady. Fun, affordable, different.

discoveries.name: Fat Choi
discoveries.notes: 4.6★/172 reviews. Vegan/vegetarian, 94 Ossington. Small but well-rated.

discoveries.name: Papi Chulo's
discoveries.notes: 4.3★/1658 reviews. Mexican/tacos, 121 Ossington — across from Golden Turtle. Casual, fun.

discoveries.name: Bobbie Sue's Mac + Cheese
discoveries.notes: 4.5★/1594 reviews. Mac & cheese specialist, 162 Ossington. Cheap, fun, Jackson-friendly.

discoveries.name: Côte de Bœuf
discoveries.notes: 4.4★/947 reviews. French butcher/wine bar, 130 Ossington. Steaks and charcuterie, more of a dinner pick.

discoveries.name: Nabe Hana
discoveries.notes: 4.9★/73 reviews. Korean hot pot, 70 Ossington. Tiny and exceptional — the sleeper pick on the strip.

discoveries.name: Bloor Street West (The Annex section)
discoveries.address: Bloor Street West, The Annex, Toronto, ON
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). One of the few North American streets that earns a mention — the Annex section from Spadina to Bathurst has the mixed use, scale, and continuous active edges that Jacobs finds absent in most of the continent.

discoveries.name: Champs-Élysées
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The most famous street in the world — and Jacobs is clear-eyed about both its glory and its decline into car traffic and brand stores.

discoveries.name: Commonwealth Avenue
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). One of the finest examples of a North American boulevard. The long tree-lined mall from the Public Garden through Back Bay is Jacobs' proof that American cities can do it right.

discoveries.name: Cours Mirabeau
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The definitive example of a European cours — the public outdoor room. Jacobs returns to it as a model for proportional perfection.

discoveries.name: Gran Vía
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). Madrid's great early 20th-century boulevard — cut through the old city fabric in three phases from 1910-1952, creating a Spanish version of Haussmann.

discoveries.name: İstiklal Caddesi
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). Istanbul's great pedestrian boulevard — Jacobs appreciates the density of use and the mix of historic arcade buildings with street life.

discoveries.name: La Rambla
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The great promenade. Jacobs' opening case study — a living room for the city, not a road.

discoveries.name: Michigan Avenue (The Magnificent Mile)
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). One of Jacobs' American examples that works — the Mag Mile as a case study in how American cities can build streets with genuine presence.

discoveries.name: Passeig de Gràcia
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The Eixample's great boulevard — Gaudí's Casa Batlló, Domènech's Casa Lleó Morera, Puig's Casa Amatller all on one block (the Block of Discord). Extraordinary streetscape.

discoveries.name: Strøget
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). One of the world's longest pedestrian streets — Jacobs uses it to explore what happens when you fully commit to walking.

discoveries.name: Unter den Linden
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The great Prussian boulevard — lined with linden trees as the name promises, connecting the Stadtschloss to the Brandenburg Gate.

discoveries.name: Via Veneto
discoveries.notes: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The street of Fellini's La Dolce Vita — Jacobs examines it as a street with character beyond its fame.
